extracted_json.title: Problem-Solving

items.text: What technologies & products should company X develop during the next 1-3 years?
How should incoming, real-time data (aka "Big Data") be used in order to design better products & services → more customer centric, more reliable,....

blocks.text: How do professionals (in Engineering, Business, Medicine, Law) solve complex problems?
Professionals use a systematic structured problem solving process (SPSP) to solve complex problems, which has 5 steps as follows:

extracted_json.summary_topic_line: Introduction to problem-solving and the structured problem solving process (SPSP) for complex problems.
extracted_json.summary_gist: The page introduces problem-solving for complex problems of the kind addressed in TIM 172A/B/P/Q. Two example problems are given: deciding what technologies and products a company should develop over the next 1-3 years, and how incoming real-time data ("Big Data") can be used to design better, more customer-centric and reliable products and services. It notes that professionals in engineering, business, medicine, and law solve complex problems using a systematic, structured problem solving process (SPSP) consisting of 5 steps.

transcription:
4.   Problem - Solving

      Complex problems ( the type of problems
      addressed in TIM 172 A/B/P/Q )

         Two examples :

      - What technologies & products should
        company X develop during the next
        1- 3 years ?

      -    How should incoming , real-time data
          (aka "Big Data") be used in order to
          design better products & services
                  ↳ more customer centric, more
                    reliable,....

      How do professionals (in Engineering,
      Business, Medicine, Law) solve complex problems?

      Professionals use a systematic structured
      problem solving process ( SPSP) to solve
      complex problems, which has 5 steps
      as follows :
